functions.name: AVPlayer.AddView
functions.signature: MBS( "AVPlayer.AddView"; AVPlayer; WindowRef; ControlName { ; DX; DY } )
functions.description: Creates a player view. We create a new player view and use it for the current player to display video data. Use AVPlayer.Release later to destroy the player and the view. If you use a register (tab controls) on the window, be aware that the text view actually is in front of the register (tab controls). You may hide the text view if another register is visible. When creating on a card window, please pass reference to parent window or just 0 for front window. On MacOS you may need to pass delta y value to adjust position. If you create a control on a new window, you may do window update command in your script first to have FileMaker actually build the window with controls and display it. A short script pause or using FM.RunScriptLater with 0.1 delay can help having all controls draw themselves. Then you can use this command to put our control on top. We use GetLayoutObjectAttribute() to query left, top, width and height and this gives empty value if control is not yet here. Mac 64-bit only.

parameters.name: AVPlayer
parameters.description: The reference number of the player.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move view more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move view more to top or bottom.

examples.label: Add view to show player:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "AVPlayer.AddView";
$$Player
; Get(WindowName); "placeholder") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVPlayer.SetControlsStyle
";
$$Player
; "Inline" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVPlayer.SetAutoResizingMask
";
$$Player
; 4 + 32 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.AddView"; $$Player; Get(WindowName); "placeholder") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.SetControlsStyle"; $$Player; "Inline" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.SetAutoResizingMask"; $$Player; 4 + 32 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVPlayer.CloseView
functions.description: Closes the video view. This function checks for a license. Created 16th March 2020 , last changed 16th March 2020 AVPlayer.AddView - AVPlayer.FindPlayers 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: AVPlayer
parameters.description: The reference number of the player.

functions.name: AVPlayer.FindPlayers
functions.description: Finds all players in current window. Looks for interactive containers using AVPlayer for playback and returns new identifiers for them. We added this function to query time of a playing interactive container on iOS. Seems to work on Mac for FileMaker 16, too. Looks on the current window with focus. If you just moved to the record, please use a short script pause to have FileMaker actually load the video.
functions.result: Returns list of identifiers or error.

examples.label: Query time of container playing container on iOS:
examples.text_code:
# find players
Set Variable [
$list
;
MBS
( "AVPlayer.FindPlayers" ) ]
# pick first one
Set Variable [
$player
; GetValue(
$list
; 1) ]
# query time
Set Variable [
$time
;
MBS
( "
AVPlayer.GetTime
"; AVPlayer ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> find players</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.FindPlayers" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> pick first one</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($list; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$player</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query time</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.GetTime"; AVPlayer ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$time</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVPlayer.GetRate
functions.description: Queries playback rate. Rate is 1.0 by default. You for example can use 0.5 to play half speed or 2.0 for double speed.

parameters.name: AVPlayer
parameters.description: The reference number of the player.

examples.label: Try the function:
examples.text_code:
# Step 1: Choose a video file
Set Variable [
$filePath
; Value:
MBS
( "FileDialog.OpenFile" ) ]
# Exit if user cancels
If [ IsEmpty(
$filePath
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Cancelled" ; "No file selected." ]
Exit Script []
End If
# Step 2: Open the file with AVPlayer and get a player reference
Set Variable [
$playerRef
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVPlayer.OpenURL
";
$filePath
) ]
# Check if the player was created
If [ IsEmpty(
$playerRef
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error" ; "Failed to create AVPlayer instance." ]
Exit Script []
End If
# Step 3: Optionally start playback (so rate is > 0)
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
AVPlayer.Play
";
$playerRef
) ]
# Add delay (so video starts playing)
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
# Step 4: Get current playback rate
Set Variable [
$rate
; Value:
MBS
( "AVPlayer.GetRate";
$playerRef
) ]
# Step 5: Show the rate
Show Custom Dialog [ "Playback Rate" ; "Current playback rate: " &
$rate
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Step 1: Choose a video file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FileDialog.OpenFile" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Exit if user cancels</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> IsEmpty($filePath) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Cancelled" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"No file selected." </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Step 2: Open the file with AVPlayer and get a player reference</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.OpenURL"; $filePath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$playerRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Check if the player was created</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> IsEmpty($playerRef) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to create AVPlayer instance." </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Step 3: Optionally start playback (so rate is > 0)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.Play"; $playerRef ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add delay (so video starts playing)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Step 4: Get current playback rate</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.GetRate"; $playerRef ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$rate</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Step 5: Show the rate</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Playback Rate" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Current playback rate: " & $rate </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVPlayer.OpenContainer
functions.description: Opens the audio file from a container. Returns the movie reference which must be released with AVPlayer.Release function. See also AVPlayer.OpenFile or AVPlayer.OpenURL . Works with normal container, compressed container and referenced container file. See WMFP.Create for Windows.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container with the audio file.

functions.result: Returns player reference number or error.

examples.label: Open from container:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$player
; Value:
MBS
("AVPlayer.OpenContainer";
AVPlayer::Container
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error loading audio file.";
$player
]
Exit Script []
End If
Set Field [
AVPlayer::Duration
;
MBS
("
AVPlayer.GetDuration
";
$player
)]
Set Field [
AVPlayer::Rate
;
MBS
("
AVPlayer.GetRate
";
$$Player
)]
Set Field [
AVPlayer::Volume
;
MBS
("
AVPlayer.GetVolume
";
$$Player
)]
Set Variable [
$$player
; Value:
$player
]
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: AVPlayer.OpenURL

functions.name: AVPlayer.Pause
functions.description: Pauses playback.

parameters.name: AVPlayer
parameters.description: The reference number of the player.

examples.label: Pause player:
examples.text_code:
If [Length(
$$Player
)>0]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("AVPlayer.Pause";
$$Player
)]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Please open Movie first!"]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [Length($$Player)>0]
	Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("AVPlayer.Pause"; $$Player)]
Else
	Show Custom Dialog ["Please open Movie first!"]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Please open Movie first!"]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [$$Player ≠ ""]
	Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS("AVPlayer.Play"; $$Player)]
Else
	Show Custom Dialog ["Please open Movie first!"]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Play with progress:
examples.text_code:
If [
$$Player
≠ "" ]
# set progress callback
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
AVPlayer.SetProgressEvaluate
";
$$Player
; "
MBS
(\"
FM.ExecuteFileSQL
\"; \"AVPlayer.fmp12\"; \"UPDATE \\\"AVPlayer\\\" SET \\\"Position\\\"=?\"; 9; 13;
$$Time
$$ / 1000)") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
AVPlayer.SetProgressInterval
";
$$Player
; 1) ]
# now plays
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("AVPlayer.Play";
$$Player
) ]
# commit. SQL can't update if record is edited.
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
# Trigger script when sound is done.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
AVPlayer.SetFinishedScript
";
$$Player
; Get(FileName); "Played") ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Please open Movie first!" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ $$Player ≠ "" ] 
	# set progress callback
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("AVPlayer.SetProgressEvaluate"; $$Player; "MBS(\"FM.ExecuteFileSQL\"; \"AVPlayer.fmp12\"; \"UPDATE \\\"AVPlayer\\\" SET \\\"Position\\\"=?\"; 9; 13; $$Time$$ / 1000)") ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("AVPlayer.SetProgressInterval"; $$Player; 1) ] 
	# now plays
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("AVPlayer.Play"; $$Player) ] 
	# commit. SQL can't update if record is edited.
	Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ] 
	# Trigger script when sound is done.
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("AVPlayer.SetFinishedScript"; $$Player; Get(FileName); "Played") ] 
Else
	Show Custom Dialog [ "Please open Movie first!" ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVPlayer.Release
functions.description: Releases the audio player. If you forget to release a player, the audio object stays in memory and if memory is low, FileMaker will crash. To avoid this the plugin will only open up to 50 files simultaneously.

parameters.name: AVPlayer
parameters.description: The reference number of the player.

examples.label: Release player:
examples.text_code:
#Close last movie if it was open.
If [Length(
$$Player
)>0]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("AVPlayer.Release";
$$Player
)]
Set Variable [
$$Player
; Value:""]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Close last movie if it was open.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>Length($$Player)>0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("AVPlayer.Release"; $$Player)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$Player</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVPlayer.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all movies in memory. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

examples.label: Cleanup:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AVPlayer.ReleaseAll" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVPlayer.SetFinishedEvaluate
functions.description: Sets expression to be run when playback is finished. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the curl reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed.

parameters.name: AVPlayer
parameters.description: The reference number of the player.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

examples.label: Sets evaluate for when player finished to show dialog:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("AVPlayer.SetFinishedEvaluate";
$Player
; "
MBS
( \"Msgbox\"; \"Player done.\" )") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("AVPlayer.SetFinishedEvaluate"; $Player; "MBS( \"Msgbox\"; \"Player done.\" )") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVPlayer.SetFinishedScript
functions.description: Sets which script to call when a transfer finished. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: AVPlayer
parameters.description: The reference number of the player.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Set finished script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("AVPlayer.SetFinishedScript"; $$Player; Get(FileName); "Played") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AVPlayer.SetFrameWithControl

parameters.name: ControlName

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move text view more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move text view more to top or bottom.

functions.name: AVPlayer.SetProgressEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to evaluate on progress. Evaluated once as second to update progress in GUI. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the curl reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed. $$Time$$ is replaced with current time in milliseconds.

parameters.name: AVPlayer
parameters.description: The reference number of the player.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

$r
; Value:
MBS
("AVPlayer.SetProgressEvaluate";
$$Player
; "
MBS
(\"
FM.ExecuteFileSQL
\"; \"AVPlayer.fmp12\"; \"UPDATE \\\"AVPlayer\\\" SET \\\"Position\\\"=?\"; 9; 13;
$$Time
$$ / 1000)") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
AVPlayer.SetProgressInterval
";
$$Player
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
AVPlayer.Play
";
$$Player
) ]
examples.xml_code:

    <Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

functions.name: AVPlayer.SetRate

functions.name: AVPlayer.SetShowsFrameSteppingButtons

functions.name: AVPlayer.SetShowsFullScreenToggleButton

parameters.name: AVPlayer

functions.name: AVPlayer.SetShowsSubtitles
functions.description: Seats whether subtitles should be shown. Set to 1 to enabled or 0 for no subtitles.

parameters.name: AVPlayer

parameters.name: Value

; Value:

( "
CNContactStore.NewContact
") ]

$r

CNContact.SetValue
";
$contactID
; "givenName"; "Joe" ) ]

; Value:

( "
CNContact.SetValue

$contactID
; "familyName"; "Miller" ) ]

$contactID
; "jobTitle"; "Family Manager" ) ]

; Value:

( "
CNContact.SetValue
";
$contactID
; "organization"; "Family" ) ]
#

$image
; Value:

( "
Container.ReadFile
"; "/Library/Desktop Pictures/Lion.jpg") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

( "
CNContact.SetValue
";
$contactID
; "imageData";
$image
) ]

; Value:

$contactID
; "test@test.test"; "Work" ) ]

$r
; Value:

CNContact.AddInstantMessage

$contactID

CNContact.AddPhoneNumber

$contactID

("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to create a contact" ;
$r

;
$contactID
]

; Value:

CNContact.JSON
";
$contactID
) ]

$json
; Value:

("
JSON.Colorize
";
MBS
( "
JSON.Format
";
$json
) ) ]
Set Field [
Contacts::Result
;
$json
]
End If

functions.name: CNContact.AddInstantMessage
functions.description: Adds an instant message account to a contact. This is a convenience function to add just one instant message account with a given label to the contact. Please call CNContactStore.UpdateContact later to store the values.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the account.

parameters.name: Service
parameters.description: The service for this account. We translate AIM, Facebook, GaduGadu, GoogleTalk, ICQ, Jabber, MSN, QQ, Skype and Yahoo to the right constant for you.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label to use. For your convenience we translate Work, Home and Other to the right values, so they get localized in display. Default label is Other.

functions.name: CNContact.AddInstantMessageJSON
functions.description: Adds an instant message account to a contact with JSON. This is a convenience function to add just one instant message account with a given label to the contact. Please call CNContactStore.UpdateContact later to store the values. The JSON object may contain the keys service and username. Service can be AIM, Facebook, GaduGadu, GoogleTalk, ICQ, Jabber, MSN, QQ, Skype or Yahoo.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label to use. For your convenience we translate Work, Home and Other to the right values, so they get localized in display. Default label is Other.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON with details

    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$json </Calculation>
    <Field table="Contacts" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CNContact.AddPostalAddress
functions.signature: MBS( "CNContact.AddPostalAddress"; ContactIdentifier { ; Label; Street; City; State; postalCode; Country; ISOCountryCode; subLocality; subAdministrativeArea } )
functions.description: Adds an postal address to a contact. This is a convenience function to add just one postal address account with a given label to the contact. Please call CNContactStore.UpdateContact later to store the values.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label to use. For your convenience we translate Work, Home and Other to the right values, so they get localized in display. Default label is Other.

parameters.name: Street
parameters.description: The street name with house number.

parameters.name: City
parameters.description: The city name.

parameters.name: State
parameters.description: The name of the state.

parameters.name: postalCode
parameters.description: The postal code.

parameters.name: Country
parameters.description: The name of the country.

parameters.name: ISOCountryCode
parameters.description: The ISO country code.

parameters.name: subLocality
parameters.description: The sub locality. Only used on MacOS 10.12.4 or iOS 10.3 and newer.

parameters.name: subAdministrativeArea
parameters.description: The sub administrative area. Only used on MacOS 10.12.4 or iOS 10.3 and newer.

functions.name: CNContact.AddPostalAddressJSON
functions.description: Adds an postal address to a contact with JSON. This is a convenience function to add just one postal address account with a given label to the contact. Please call CNContactStore.UpdateContact later to store the values. The JSON should be an object with a few of the following keys: street, city, state, postalCode, country and ISOCountryCode. For MacOS 10.12.4 or iOS 10.3, you can set subLocality and subAdministrativeArea, too.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label to use. For your convenience we translate Work, Home and Other to the right values, so they get localized in display. Default label is Other.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The block of JSON.

functions.name: CNContact.AddRelation
functions.description: Adds an relation to a contact. This is a convenience function to add just one relation with a given label to the contact. Please call CNContactStore.UpdateContact later to store the values.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name to use.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label to use. For your convenience we translate Assistant, Brother, Child, Daughter, Father, Friend, Manager, Mother, Parent, Partner, Sister, Son and Spouse to the right values, so they get localized in display. Default label is Other.

functions.name: CNContact.AddSocialProfile
functions.signature: MBS( "CNContact.AddSocialProfile"; ContactIdentifier; Label; URLString; UserName; UserIdentifier; Service )
functions.description: Adds an social profile to a contact. This is a convenience function to add just one social profile with a given label to the contact. Please call CNContactStore.UpdateContact later to store the values.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label to use. For your convenience we translate Work, Home and Other to the right values, so they get localized in display. Default label is Other.

parameters.name: URLString
parameters.description: The URL of the service.

parameters.name: UserName
parameters.description: The user name.

parameters.name: UserIdentifier
parameters.description: The user idenitifer.

parameters.name: Service
parameters.description: The service name. For your convenience we accept here Facebook, Flickr, LinkedIn, MySpace, SinaWeibo, TencentWeibo, Twitter, Yelp and GameCenter to the correct constant.

functions.name: CNContact.AddSocialProfileJSON
functions.description: Adds an social profile to a contact with JSON. This is a convenience function to add just one social profile with a given label to the contact. Please call CNContactStore.UpdateContact later to store the values.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label to use. For your convenience we translate Work, Home and Other to the right values, so they get localized in display. Default label is Other.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON with the object containing the fields urlString, username, userIdentifier and service.

functions.name: CNContact.AddURLAddress
functions.description: Adds an URL address to a contact. This is a convenience function to add just one URL with a given label to the contact. Please call CNContactStore.UpdateContact later to store the values.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The URL address.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label to use. For your convenience we translate Homepage, Work, Home and Other to the right values, so they get localized in display. Default label is Other.

functions.name: CNContact.DeleteValue
functions.description: Deletes a value of a contact. Selector can be birthday, contactRelations, dates, departmentName, emailAddresses, familyName, givenName, imageData, imageDataAvailable, instantMessageAddresses, jobTitle, middleName, namePrefix, nameSuffix, nickname, nonGregorianBirthday, organizationName, phoneNumbers, phoneticFamilyName, phoneticGivenName, phoneticMiddleName, phoneticOrganizationName, postalAddresses, previousFamilyName, socialProfiles, thumbnailImageData, note, type, ImageData and thumbnailImageData or urlAddresses. PhoneNumbers, emailAddresses, postalAddresses, urlAddresses, contactRelations, socialProfiles, instantMessageAddresses and dates can specify a label/identifier to delete only one entry.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to set.

parameters.name: Identifier
parameters.description: Identifier or label to delete only one entry in a list. Pass empty text to delete all.

examples.label: Query names for all contacts:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of contact IDs
	list = MBS( "CNContactStore.Contacts"; 1 ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	id = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query name for each ID
	name = MBS( "CNContact.Name"; id); 
	names = names & name & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CNContact.SetValue
functions.description: Sets the value of a contact. Selector can be birthday, contactRelations, dates, departmentName, emailAddresses, familyName, givenName, imageData, imageDataAvailable, instantMessageAddresses, jobTitle, middleName, namePrefix, nameSuffix, nickname, nonGregorianBirthday, organizationName, phoneNumbers, phoneticFamilyName, phoneticGivenName, phoneticMiddleName, phoneticOrganizationName, postalAddresses, previousFamilyName, socialProfiles, thumbnailImageData, note, type or urlAddresses. ImageData and thumbnailImageData are passed as containers. PhoneNumbers, emailAddresses, postalAddresses, urlAddresses, contactRelations, socialProfiles, instantMessageAddresses, dates, birthday and nonGregorianBirthday are passed as JSON. See also Apple's documentation: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/contacts/cncontact For iOS 13 or later or macOS 16 or later, the note property can't be set or read unless the application has permissions for that and FileMaker Pro doesn't have these. AppleScript may work around this.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to set.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to set.

examples.label: Sets the note field for a contact via AppleScript functions:
examples.text_code:
# Set Contact Note via AppleScript
Set Variable [
$theID
; Value: "773E1A42-ABCD-4CCF-88DE-E9C64BEE6D33:ABPerson" ]
Set Variable [
$theNote
; Value: "Best Hotdogs in town!" ]
# Compile AppleScript
Set Variable [
$ScriptID
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.Compile
"; "property theID : \"\"¶  property theNote : \"\"¶ tell application \"Contacts\"¶ 	set theContact to the first person whose id is theID¶ 	set note of theContact to theNote¶ 	save¶ 	return note of theContact¶ end tell" ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Result from AppleScript" ;
MBS
( "
AppleScript.LastError
" ) & ¶ &
MBS
( "
AppleScript.LastErrorMessage
" ) ]
# run a query or multiple
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.SetPropertyValue
";
$ScriptID
; "theID";
$theID
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.SetPropertyValue
";
$ScriptID
; "theNote";
$theNote
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ScriptID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">

    <Message>

      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> run a query or multiple</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AppleScript.SetPropertyValue"; $ScriptID; "theID"; $theID ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AppleScript.SetPropertyValue"; $ScriptID; "theNote"; $theNote ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "AppleScript.Execute"; $ScriptID) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Result from AppleScript" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$result & ¶ & MBS( "AppleScript.LastError" ) & ¶ & MBS( "AppleScript.LastErrorMessage" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>

      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AppleScript.Close"; $ScriptID) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CNContact.Show
functions.description: Shows a contact as sheet. Shows Cancel and Edit buttons to work on the contact. Does currently not work on MacOS due to bugs in Apple frameworks. Implemented for macOS as popover in v12.5.

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

examples.label: Show contact:
examples.text_code:
# show a contact by ID (iOS only currently)
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "CNContact.Show";
PlaceHolder::ContactID
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to show contact" ;
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> show a contact by ID (iOS only currently)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContact.Show"; PlaceHolder::ContactID ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to show contact" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CNContact.Value
functions.description: Queries the value of contact. Selector can be birthday, contactRelations, dates, departmentName, emailAddresses, familyName, givenName, imageData, imageDataAvailable, instantMessageAddresses, jobTitle, middleName, namePrefix, nameSuffix, nickname, nonGregorianBirthday, organizationName, phoneNumbers, phoneticFamilyName, phoneticGivenName, phoneticMiddleName, phoneticOrganizationName, postalAddresses, previousFamilyName, socialProfiles, thumbnailImageData, note, type or urlAddresses. ImageData and thumbnailImageData are returned as container and you can pass in ImageType and FileName to use. PhoneNumbers, emailAddresses, postalAddresses, urlAddresses, contactRelations, socialProfiles, instantMessageAddresses, dates, birthday and nonGregorianBirthday are returned as JSON. See also Apple's documentation: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/contacts/cncontact

parameters.name: ContactIdentifier
parameters.description: The contact identifier.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to return.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: Files.AccessDate
functions.description: Queries the access date of the file or folder. Although the function is named date, it does return the time, too. Result is a timestamp. May include seconds with fractions, if supported on the file system. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the file to check.

functions.name: Files.AppPath

Use Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath to convert to a FileMaker path if you plan to use "filemac:" on a built in FileMaker function. Returns on FileMaker Cloud (Linux) usually this path: "/opt/FileMaker/FileMaker Server/Database Server/bin/fmsased"

functions.name: Files.ApplicationPathWithBundleIdentifier

This returns "" if no app has the bundle identifier. Pass 1 for second parameter to get a list with all paths. This functions looks up the database from macOS and doesn't search the disk.

functions.name: Files.AudioCoverArt
functions.description: Loads audio file and returns cover art. Should work for MP3, MP4 and FLAC files. May return JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP depending on what the file contains. Result can be assigned to container. Result is empty, if there is no art found. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path to the audio file.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The desired file name.

; Value: "/Users/cs/Music/test.mp3" ]
Set Field [

; Value:

("Files.AudioCoverArt";
$path
) ]

  </Step>

functions.name: Files.AudioTags
functions.description: Queries audio tags for audio file. Returns JSON object with usual two sub objects. One is AudioProperties with length (in seconds), bitrate (in bits per second), sampleRate (in Hz) and channels as numbers. The Properties object contains all the properties with various tags. Values are texts for the given keys. Uses TagLib, an open source library to read metadata from audio files, e.g. ID3 tags. Currently it supports both ID3v1 and ID3v2 for MP3 files, Ogg Vorbis comments and ID3 tags and Vorbis comments in FLAC, MPC, Speex, WavPack, TrueAudio, WAV, AIFF, MP4 and ASF files. List of ID3v2 tag names: ALBUM, ALBUMARTIST, ALBUMARTISTSORT, ALBUMSORT, ARTIST, ARTISTSORT, ARTISTWEBPAGE, AUDIOSOURCEWEBPAGE, BPM, COMMENT, COMPOSER, CONDUCTOR, CONTENTGROUP, COPYRIGHT, COPYRIGHTURL, DATE, DISCNUMBER, ENCODEDBY, ENCODING, ENCODINGTIME, FILETYPE, FILEWEBPAGE, GENRE, INITIALKEY, INVOLVEDPEOPLE, ISRC, LABEL, LANGUAGE, LENGTH, LYRICIST, LYRICS, MEDIA, MOOD, MUSICIANCREDITS, ORIGINALALBUM, ORIGINALARTIST, ORIGINALDATE, ORIGINALFILENAME, ORIGINALLYRICIST, OWNER, PAYMENTWEBPAGE, PLAYLISTDELAY, PODCAST, PODCASTCATEGORY, PODCASTDESC, PODCASTID, PODCASTURL, PRODUCEDNOTICE, PUBLISHERWEBPAGE, RADIOSTATION, RADIOSTATIONOWNER, RADIOSTATIONWEBPAGE, RELEASEDATE, REMIXER, SUBTITLE, TAGGINGDATE, TITLE, TITLESORT, TRACKNUMBER, URL. See Files.AudioCoverArt to get covert art. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path to the audio file.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to dump ID3v2Tags values. Default is 0 for no flags.

examples.label: Query MP3 tags:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("Files.AudioTags"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.mp3")
Example result:

"AudioProperties"
:	{
"length"
:
282
,

:
128
,

44100
,

:
2
},
"Tags"
:	{
"ARTIST"
:
"John Doe"
,
"COMMENT"
:
"test comment"
,
"TITLE"
:
"Hello World"
}
}

examples.label: Colorize and format in Auto-Enter calculation:

Let ( [
jsonFormatted =
MBS
("
JSON.Format
"; Self);
jsonFormatFailed =
MBS
("IsError");
jsonText = If(jsonFormatFailed; Self; jsonFormatted);
result =
MBS
("
JSON.Colorize
"; jsonText)
]; result )
// put this calculation into a field, so the field gets formated and colorized whenever the field is changed and the record gets commited.

result = MBS("JSON.Colorize"; jsonText)
]; result )

// put this calculation into a field, so the field gets formated and colorized whenever the field is changed and the record gets commited.</Calculation>
    </Value>

functions.name: Files.CanOpenFile
functions.description: Checks if plugin can read file. The plugin tries to open file exclusive. If that fails another application or FileMaker has the file open. Returns 1 if open was okay, 0 if file is busy or an error like permissions denied. If you just need to know whether you can open a file, you can just read it. Or test by opening with BinaryFile.Open function. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file.

examples.label: Check if file exists:

    </Message>
    <Buttons>

        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.CopyFile
functions.signature: MBS( "Files.CopyFile"; SourcePath; DestPath { ; Overwrite; SkipSourcePermissionErrors; ProgressTitle } )
functions.description: Copies files and folders without user interface. SourcePath and DestPath must be valid and exist. So pass destination folder for the destination. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path. The Linux implementation copies file to dest. You need to have read permissions for source file. We try to preserve extended attributes and file permissions. On Windows the 5th parameter can be 1 to use alternative copy function from Windows. This one is for copying one file only, but may be more successful in some cases.

parameters.name: SourcePath
parameters.description: The native path to the file or folder to be copied.

parameters.name: DestPath
parameters.description: The native path to the destination folder or file.

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.description: Mac only: Whether to allow overwriting existing files. Default is off. Pass 1 to enable and 0 to disable. (ignored on other platforms)

parameters.name: SkipSourcePermissionErrors
parameters.description: Mac only: Whether to skip files with permission errors. Default is off. Pass 1 to enable and 0 to disable.

parameters.name: ProgressTitle
parameters.description: Title for the progress window. For Windows only and the text may not be visible in your version of Windows.

functions.name: Files.CreateAlias
functions.signature: MBS( "Files.CreateAlias"; AliasFilePath; TargetPath { ; RelativeTo or WorkingPath; IconPathAndIndex } )
functions.description: Creates an alias or link file. If relative path is provided, the alias will include the relative path from that folder to the target item. When resolved later and the full path is invalid, the relative path may find it. See Files.CreateSymbolicLink for creating symbol links and Files.CreateHardLink for hard links. Testing in macOS Catalina shows for us, that the icon can be added, but the Finder does not display it. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: AliasFilePath
parameters.description: The path for the alias file path.

parameters.name: TargetPath
parameters.description: The target path to have the alias point to.

parameters.name: RelativeTo or WorkingPath
parameters.description: The folder path to use relative resolution for Mac or the working directory for Windows.

parameters.name: IconPathAndIndex
parameters.description: The icon file path to use. Optionally with an index as second item in a value list. For Windows must reference an exe, ico, dll or similar file containing resources with icons. For MacOS a path to an image file where we load image and use it for the icon.

functions.name: Files.CreateBookmark
functions.description: Creates a bookmark from a file path. This is like an alias file in Finder, but as text, so you can store it in a text field in your database.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to file or folder where the bookmark refers to.

parameters.name: RelativePath
parameters.description: Optional the path of a file/folder being the relative path. This can be the native path to your database. This way you can later find the file again if database and target file moved together.

parameters.name: PreferFileIDResolution
parameters.description: Optional, whether you prefer FileID resolution later over path resolution. Can be 1 or 0. Default is 0.

parameters.name: MinimalBookmark
parameters.description: Optional, whether to request a minimal bookmark. Creates bookmark data with "less" information, which may be smaller but still be able to resolve in certain ways.

functions.result: Returns bookmark data text or error message.

functions.name: Files.CreateDirectory
functions.description: Creates a directory. Returns "OK" if the directory already exists. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path. For MacOS & iOS creates folders recursively as needed. In the Mac world we call it a folder, in the Windows world a directory. This function works cross platform and got the name with Directory years ago. You can use Files.DeleteFolder to delete a folder with its content. Changed for plugin version 10.5 to do recursive folder creation on Windows, too.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the new directory to be created.

examples.label: Create folder on Desktop:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$folder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" )]
If [IsEmpty(
Container Export::Name
)]
Set Field [
Container Export::Name
; "Record " & Get(RecordID)]
End If
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$folder
;
Container Export::Name
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Files.CreateDirectory";
$path
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.UserDesktop" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$folder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>IsEmpty(Container Export::Name)</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Record " & Get(RecordID)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Container Export" id="99999" name="Name"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $folder; Container Export::Name )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.CreateDirectory"; $path)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.CreationDate
functions.description: Queries the creation date of the file or folder. Although the function is named date, it does return the time, too. Result is a timestamp. May include seconds with fractions, if supported on the file system. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the file to check.

examples.label: Queries creation date of a file
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.CreationDate"; "C:\test.txt" )</Calculation>

functions.name: Files.Delete
functions.description: Deletes a file directly without moving it to trash. Works also for directories and can easily remove whole directory hierarchies! Be careful and make backups. Use Files.MoveToTrash to move files to trash which is often better as it allows users to undo the operation. Also Files.MoveToTrash usually works on files in use. It is possible that the OS decides to not delete the file and fail (e.g. due to permission errors). Version 6.3 and newer support deleting a list of files. So you can pass a list of paths instead of just one. Stops at the first error. Warning: If you pass path to a folder hierarchy, the whole folder hierarchy gets deleted! On Windows you can't delete/move an open file. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to be deleted.

functions.name: Files.DeleteFolder
functions.description: Deletes a file or folder directly without moving to trash. Use Files.MoveToTrash to move files to trash which is often better as it allows users to undo the operation. Also Files.MoveToTrash usually works on files in use. It is possible that the OS decides to not delete the file and fail (e.g. due to permission errors). Folders are deleted recursively. On Linux in v13.0 or newer, we try to delete the folder. If that fails, because the file is not empty, we will recursively delete the content. Once that is done, we try again. If something failed to delete inside the folder, the function will report the error about directory not being empty. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native folder path to the item to be deleted.

functions.name: Files.DeleteLater
functions.description: Deletes file later. We keep a list of file paths and delete them when FileMaker quits. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path.

examples.label: Write temp file and launch it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$filename
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.FileName
"; GetValue(GetAsText(MyTable:: Photo); 1)) ]
# get temp file path
Set Variable [
$filepath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserTemporary
" );
$filename
) ]
# write it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Container.WriteFile
";
MyTable::Photo
;
$filepath
) ]
# delete later
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Files.DeleteLater";
$filepath
) ]
# launch it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Files.LaunchFile
";
$filepath
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.FileName"; GetValue(GetAsText(MyTable:: Photo); 1)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filename</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get temp file path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; MBS( "Folders.UserTemporary" ); $filename) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filepath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.WriteFile"; MyTable::Photo; $filepath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> delete later</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.DeleteLater"; $filepath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> launch it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.LaunchFile"; $filepath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.DirectoryExists
functions.description: Checks whether a directory exists with the given path. If item exists at the path Files.ItemExists returns 1. If it's a directory, Files.DirectoryExists also returns 1 and Files.FileExists returns 0. With a file, Files.FileExists returns 1 and Files.DirectoryExists returns 0. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the item to check.

functions.result: Returns 1 if directory exists and 0 if not.

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Mode

parameters.name: PathToVolume
parameters.description: The path to volume. e.g. C:\, \\MyServer\MyDisk\ or /Volumes/testDisk/ or / for root volume on macOS.

";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserTemporary
" ); "
MBS
.dll") ]
# write DLL file to disk

Install Plugin Update if needed::Plugin

    </Repetition>

    <Repetition>

    <Name>$r</Name>

functions.name: Files.FileKind
functions.description: Queries the file kind string. This is the file type displayed by the Finder or Explorer. Like "FileMaker Pro Database" for a .fp7 file. This string can be localized to the current language of the user. On server always English as that is the locale used by default.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the file to check.

functions.result: Returns file kind text or error.

examples.label: Queries file kind of a file
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.FileKind"; "C:\test.txt" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.FileSize
functions.description: Queries file size. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path. With 2.9 plugins you can set parameter to query only size of resource fork or data fork on macOS. And you can define if you want to query size on disk (rounded up to block size) or the logical size.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the file to check.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Optionally specifies on macOS what size is returned. Default is total logical file size. Pass datafork or resourcefork to limit query to only size of given fork. And pass physical or logical to query logical size or the physical size, defined by blocks used on Disk. Pass combination of total, physical, datafork, logical, resourcefork.

examples.label: Queries file size of a file
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query all file names and sizes for files on desktop:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of visible files on desktop
	folder = MBS("Folders.UserDesktop");
	list = MBS( "Files.List"; folder; 1+4 ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	filename = GetValue(list; index); 
	filepath = MBS("Path.AddPathComponent"; folder; filename);
	// query size for each file
	size = MBS( "Files.FileSize"; filepath); 
	names = names & filename & " -> " & size & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.FileUTITypes
functions.description: Queries uniform type identifiers for this file. For example a rtf file has the type public.rtf and going up the chain of types, it also has "public.text, public.data, public.item and public.content. A jpg image has types public.jpeg, public.image, public.data, public.item and public.content. So if you look for "public.image" in the types, you can identify that the file is an image file.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the file to check.

functions.result: Returns file types as text or error.

examples.label: Queries types of a file
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.FolderSize
functions.description: Calculate sizes of folder. On success returns a list of values. Please calculate ones, save result in variable and pick the values you need. It's quite efficient for us to query all those values in one run as disk access is the slowest part here, not summing up the values.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native file path to folder.

parameters.name: RecursionLimit
parameters.description: The limit for the recursion. Default is -1 for no limit. Pass zero to only look in files in that folder and not subfolders.

parameters.name: IgnoreHiddenFolderContent
parameters.description: Do not count items in hidden subfolders. Pass 1 to ignore or 0 to count. Default is 0.

parameters.name: CountBundlesAsItem
parameters.description: Count bundles as items. Default is 0 to look inside those bundles, e.g. apps. Pass 1 to count bundles as files.

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.GetComment
functions.description: Queries Finder's file comment. Use Apple Events internally and may fail if those are not permitted. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the file.

This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

functions.name: Files.GetNodeID
functions.description: Queries the NodeID of the file or folder. On macOS, every file usually has an unique ID on the hard disk. Not all volumes support NodeIDs, but if they do, you can later find the file back on that volume using Files.GetPathFromNodeID . Added Windows support in plugin version 8.5. While node id is currently a 64-bit integer, it could change in future to an UUID or 128bit number. Added iOS and Linux support in version 16.0. Sorry, but Linux has no good way to find a file by id except

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file/folder path.

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.GetPathFromNodeID
functions.description: Finds the file or folder for the given NodeID on the given volume. You need to pass the path to the volume where this NodeID belongs to. This does not work well for most network mounts. The plugin may run a search which can take a while. Added Windows support in plugin version 8.5. Added Linux and iOS support in version 16.1.

parameters.name: VolumePath
parameters.description: The native path to the volume. On Linux please put in the start path for the recursive search.

parameters.name: NodeID
parameters.description: The node ID for the file, a number.

functions.result: Returns the path for the file or error message.

examples.label: Resolve node on system volume:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.GetPathFromNodeID"; "/"; $NodeID )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.GetPosixPermissions
functions.description: Queries posix file permissions. Path must be valid and exist. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path. See chmod command manual page. The plugin converts parameter to string and parses it as octal number. This way you can pass 777 as number and it's interpreted as expected.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the file or folder.

# shows for example 755</Calculation>

functions.name: Files.GetTagNames

Please note that some tags may include chr(10) followed by a number to indicate which label color is used for compatibility to older OS X versions. Those appear escaped as \n in the result. You can use Files.GetFinderLabel to read label number.

functions.name: Files.IsExcludedFromBackup
functions.description: Queries file extension visibility state of a file or folder. Value is 1 if resource should be excluded from backups, 0 otherwise. This property is only useful for excluding cache and other application support files which are not needed in a backup. Some operations commonly made to user documents will cause this property to be reset to false and so this property should not be used on user documents. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

functions.name: Files.ItemExists

If it's a directory, Files.DirectoryExists also returns 1 and Files.FileExists returns 0. With a file, Files.FileExists returns 1 and Files.DirectoryExists returns 0. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path.

parameters.name: Path

functions.result: Returns 1 if item exists and 0 if not.

functions.name: Files.Launch
functions.description: Launches the file. The OS will lookup what application is associated with the file type and launch the application asking it to open the document. You can use this function to open a database file with FileMaker or your runtime solution. Query with Files.AppPath the path to your application. Then call Launch with that application path and your database file path. Please use Files.LaunchFile to open a file and Files.Launch to open a file in a specific application. Version 5.3 supports passing several DocPath parameters. With version 7.2 the last parameter can be a number with 0 or 1 to define whether to run a new app instance. Check also the Shell functions to run command line tools and get the output. This may work on the server, where the normal launch function may not work due to missing GUI. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat DocPath parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: AppPath
parameters.description: Native path to the application to launch.

parameters.name: DocPath...
parameters.description: Optional, Native path to the document to pass as argument. Must be text to distingush from flag, which are numbers.

parameters.name: NewInstance
parameters.description: Whether to launch new app instance on Mac. Default is 0.

$dbpath
; Value:Get ( FilePath )]

( "
Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath
";
$dbpath
)]
Set Variable [

functions.name: Files.LaunchFile
functions.description: Launches the file or opens folder. The OS will lookup what application is associated with the file type and launch the application asking it to open the document. See also Files.Launch if you need to pass application and document. If you launch a folder path, the folder will open in Finder/Explorer. Please use Files.LaunchFile to open a file and Files.Launch to open a file in a specific application. Check also the Shell functions to run command line tools and get the output. This may work on the server, where the normal launch function may not work due to missing GUI. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

examples.label: Write container to temp file and launch:
examples.text_code:
# get temp folder
Set Variable [
$TempFolder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.SystemTemporary
" )]
# and file name from container
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
test::myContainer
)]
# add name to folder path
Set Variable [
$TempPath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$TempFolder
;
$name
)]
# now write temp file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.WriteFile
";
test::myContainer
;
$TempPath
)]
# and launch file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Files.LaunchFile";
$TempPath
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get temp folder</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.SystemTemporary" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TempFolder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and file name from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; test::myContainer )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add name to folder path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $TempFolder; $name )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TempPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now write temp file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.WriteFile"; test::myContainer; $TempPath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and launch file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.LaunchFile"; $TempPath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Launch Script taking a list of files to launch:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
#
# loop over file paths received
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: ValueCount(
$list
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 1 ]
If [
$index
≤
$count
]
Loop
# launch it
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: GetValue(
$list
;
$index
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Files.LaunchFile";
$path
) ]
# wait half a second
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
#
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
>
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over file paths received</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount($list) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index ≤ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> launch it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($list; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.LaunchFile"; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> wait half a second</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,5 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.List
functions.description: Queries list of file names in folder. The list of names is separated by new line character. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path. With filter parameter being 8 on Windows, you can pass a path with wildcards directly and ExtensionFilter parameter is ignored. This is not supported on macOS. See also Files.ListRecursive . You can filter result with List.MatchesPostfix to get list with one file extension only. Our plugin does not sort and return the files in the order the OS returned them. If you need sorting, please check List.Sort function. Also use List.RemovePrefix or List.RemovePostfix to filter list further. On macOS, if you can't read a directory, please also check privacy system settings for whether FileMaker has permissions to look into desktop, documents or downloads folder or remote volumes.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the directory.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Optional, limits what is returned. Default is 0 to report all files. Pass a combination of the following values: 1 for showing only files, 2 for showing only directories and 4 for showing only visible items. 8 if the path includes the file name specification with wildcards; a.k.a glob. 128 to use the extension filter text and do a substring match on the file name. e.g. pass 4+1 for visible files or 2+4 for visible directories.

parameters.name: ExtensionFilter
parameters.description: Optional, a filter for getting only files with matching file extension. Since version 16.1 this can be a list.

functions.result: Returns list of files separated by return.

examples.label: Query all file names and sizes for files on desktop:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of visible files on desktop
	folder = MBS("Folders.UserDesktop");
	list = MBS( "Files.List"; folder; 1+4 ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	filename = GetValue(list; index); 
	filepath = MBS("Path.AddPathComponent"; folder; filename);
	// query size for each file
	size = MBS( "Files.FileSize"; filepath); 
	names = names & filename & " -> " & size & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

# list files in a folder
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Files.List";
$FolderPath
) ]
# loop over the files
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: ValueCount (
$r
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$count
>0 ]
Loop
# build path
Set Variable [
$fileName
; GetValue (
$r
;
$index
+1  )]
Set Variable [

; Value:

( "

";
$FolderPath
;
$fileName
)]
Set Variable [

; Value:

("
Container.ReadFile
";
$filePath
)]
# create record for new file
New Record/Request
Set Field [
MyData::Container
;
$fileData
]
Set Field [
MyData::Name
;
$fileName
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
=
$count
]
End Loop
End If
