<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> list files in a folder</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.List"; $FolderPath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over the files</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $r ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count>0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> build path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue ( $r ; $index+1  )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fileName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $FolderPath; $fileName )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadFile"; $filePath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fileData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> create record for new file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$fileData </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyData" id="99999" name="Container"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$fileName </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyData" id="99999" name="Name"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index = $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.ListAsJSON
functions.description: Queries list of file names in folder as JSON. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path. With filter parameter being 8 on Windows, you can pass a path with wildcards directly and ExtensionFilter parameter is ignored. This is not supported on macOS. TimeStamps are returned in the FileMaker formatting. On Windows a key AlternateFileName may be included in result if the file has an alternative file name for DOS. If you need sorting, please check JSON.SortWithEvaluate function. We can add more Keys if needed. Let us know if you need another value for all files.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the directory.

parameters.name: Keys
parameters.description: The keys to retrieve. Pass a list of values including Size, CreationTimeStamp, AccessTimeStamp, ModificationTimeStamp, Visible. Name, Directory and Type can be passed, but are always included. If keys is "All", we include all keys. Added IsApplication and IsPackage values for v13.5.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Optional, limits what is returned. Default is 0 to report all files. Pass a combination of the following values: 1 for showing only files, 2 for showing only directories and 4 for showing only visible items. 8 if the path includes the file name specification with wildcards. 128 to use the extension filter text and do a substring match on the file name. e.g. pass 4+1 for visible files or 2+4 for visible directories.

parameters.name: ExtensionFilter
parameters.description: Optional, a filter for getting only files with matching file extension. Since version 16.1 this can be a list.

functions.name: Files.ListRecursive
functions.description: Queries list of paths in folder. This function is recursive and will look in all subfolders. Due to very large file systems you can pass a timeout. Default is 5 seconds. FileMaker will not respond to user input while the query runs. The list of names is separated by new line character. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path. You can decide on Mac on whether files inside a package (e.g. application) are listed or not. By default we ignore those files, but you can include package content with adding 16 to the filter parameter. See also Files.List . If input path ends with / on Windows, we use slash for adding path components in v8.4 or newer. Otherwise we use the default on Windows, which is a backslash. You can filter by file extensions when doing the list and then filter with List.MatchesPostfix function.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the directory.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Optional, limits what is returned. Default is 0 to report all files. Pass a combination of the following values: 1 for showing only files, 2 for showing only directories and 4 for showing only visible files. 16 to include package file content on Mac. 32 to treat packages as files. 64 to not list content of invisible directories. 128 to use the extension filter text and do a substring match on the file name.

parameters.name: TimeOut
parameters.description: the timeout in seconds. Default is 5.

parameters.name: ExtensionFilter
parameters.description: Optional, a filter for getting only files with matching file extension. Since version 16.1 this can be a list.

functions.result: Returns list of files separated by return.

functions.name: Files.ModificationDate
functions.description: Queries the modification date of the file or folder. Although the function is named date, it does return the time, too. Result is a timestamp. May include seconds with fractions, if supported on the file system. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path

functions.name: Files.Mount
functions.signature: MBS( "Files.Mount"; URL { ; Dest; UserName; Password; Interactive; Prompt } )
functions.description: Mounts a network share. Use on Server may require extra work as server runs with a different user account than you being logged in on the server machine. Permissions don't always allow things to be done. When used on Linux the parameters are a little bit different: Please pass source (URL), target (Dest), filesystemtype (Username) and data (Password). Values for the filesystemtype argument supported by the kernel are listed in /proc/filesystems (e.g., "btrfs", "ext4", "jfs", "xfs", "vfat", "fuse", "tmpfs", "cgroup", "proc", "mqueue", "nfs", "cifs", "iso9660").  Further types may become available when the appropriate modules are loaded. The data argument is interpreted by the different filesystems. Typically it is a string of comma-separated options understood by this filesystem. The dest path may need to be created first as new folder.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The URL for the network resource.

parameters.name: Dest
parameters.description: The destination path to mount to. If you leave this empty, the system decides. On Mac this can be the path to a folder where to mount new disk inside, e.g. to make a private mount.

parameters.name: UserName
parameters.description: The user name. If none is provided, system can take credentials from keychain.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The password to use.

parameters.name: Interactive
parameters.description: Default is 0 for no dialogs. But you can pass 1 to have a dialog show up.

parameters.name: Prompt
parameters.description: Pass 1 on Windows to show dialog always to enter password.

functions.name: Files.MountPath
functions.description: Queries mount path for a volume. For a network mounted disk, you can query the original mount point. May not work on Mac for AFP shares, but for SMB shares. Returns empty path if no path is known. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Disk
parameters.description: Path native path to disk drive.

functions.result: Returns mount URL, empty or error.

functions.name: Files.MoveFile
functions.signature: MBS( "Files.MoveFile"; SourcePath; DestPath { ; Overwrite; SkipSourcePermissionErrors; ProgressTitle } )
functions.description: Moves a file or folder without user interface. SourcePath and DestPath must be valid and exist. So pass destination folder for the destination. The Linux implementation does only move files within the same file system. On Mac you may get error -43 for file not found if you refer to a file in a folder, where you don't have file permissions to look inside the folder. See also Files.RenameFile to rename a file. On Windows, if the destination path is relative, it is applied to the current process' working directory. See Process.SetCurrentDirectory to change that. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: SourcePath
parameters.description: The native path to the file or folder to be moved.

parameters.name: DestPath
parameters.description: The native path to the destination folder or file.

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.description: Mac only: Whether to allow overwriting existing files. Default is off. Pass 1 to enable and 0 to disable. On Windows, it overwrites always.

parameters.name: SkipSourcePermissionErrors
parameters.description: Mac only: Whether to skip files with permission errors. Default is off. Pass 1 to enable and 0 to disable.

parameters.name: ProgressTitle
parameters.description: Title for the progress window. For Windows only and the text may not be visible in your version of Windows.

examples.label: Move a file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.MoveFile"; "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\test1.txt"; "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\test2.txt")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.MoveToTrash
functions.description: Deletes an item by moving it to trash without user interface. Use this functions to move files and folders to the trash. It is possible that the OS decides to not delete the file and fail (e.g. due to permission errors) or that the medium does not have a trash, so the file is deleted right away. On Windows you can't delete/move an open file. If you delete a file in a server script on a macOS server, the item is moved into the /Library/FileMaker Server/.Trash folder. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file or folder to be deleted.

functions.name: Files.PrintFile

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

functions.name: Files.ReadFile
functions.description: Reads a file with the given path. Reads the data in the file and returns as text with native encoding. This may truncate the string at the first null byte. You can optionally define a mode like base64 or Hex to read any bytes including null bytes. See also Text.ReadTextFile for reading text files with specific encoding. Reading files over 1 GB is not supported. Use Container.ReadFile for bigger files. Added new modes with version 5.3: auto, compressed, pdf, png, jpg, tif, gif, bmp or psd. For reading simple text files, please use Text.ReadTextFile . See also specialized functions Files.ReadJPEG , Files.ReadPDF , Files.ReadPNG , Text.ReadTextFile and RichText.ReadFile . For PDF content: If you have a DynaPDF Pro license and DynaPDF functions initialized, this function can add previews for the PDF file on Windows and Linux. For MacOS, we make them with PDFKit. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path. Something like "/Users/cs/desktop/test.txt" on Mac and "C:\Programs\Data\test.txt" on Windows. Files without path end in the root directory on Mac.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: auto: to auto detect the type of container based on the file name. base64: pass base64 to encode data first to base64 encoding. Base64URL: Base64URL encoding. Hex: Can be Hex to encode to hex encoding. container: the result is returned as a container with a FILE stream inside. jpg: Return as JPEG container. png: Return as PNG container. tif: Return as TIFF container. gif: Return as GIF container. bmp: Return as BMP container. psd: Return as PSD container. pdf: Return as PDF container. This may calculate a preview image. Default is return content as text if you pass "" or ommit the parameter.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: If mode is container, this defines the file name to use.

examples.label: Read file with some data:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.ReadFile"; $Path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.xml_code:

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. Files in standard temporary folders do not need to be marked for exclusion as those are automatically excluded.

; Value: "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\test.txt" ]
# check if file is marked read only
Set Variable [
$isReadOnly
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.IsReadOnly
";
$path
) ]
If [
$isReadOnly
= 1 ]
# remove read only flag
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Files.SetReadOnly";
$path
; 0) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\test.txt" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check if file is marked read only</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.IsReadOnly"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$isReadOnly</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $isReadOnly = 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> remove read only flag</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.SetReadOnly"; $path; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Files.SetSystemImmutable
functions.description: Sets the system immutable flag. You may not be able to set it from FileMaker unless FileMaker runs with evaluated permissions.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the file or directory.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new flag value. Pass 1 or 0.

functions.name: Files.SetTagNames
functions.description: Sets the tags for a file/folder. Requires macOS 10.9. This function needs a native path. Please use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath function in order to convert a FileMaker style path to a native path. Please note that some tags may include Char(10) followed by a number to indicate which label color is used for compatibility to older OS X versions. Those should appear escaped as \n in the taglist.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the file.

parameters.name: Taglist
parameters.description: The list of tags names, separated by newline character.

functions.name: Files.SetUserImmutable
functions.description: Sets the user immutable flag. This is the lock checkbox in Finder's information window.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the file or directory.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new flag value. Pass 1 or 0.

    </Repetition>

This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

functions.name: Files.WriteFile
functions.description: Writes a file with the given data. You provide either a container value or some text. For text you can optionally define a filter like base64 or Hex. See also Text.WriteTextFile for writing text files with specific encoding. See FileDialog functions if you like to show a save dialog to pick a destination for the file path. If you need to create directory first, please use Files.CreateDirectory function. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The data to save. Can be text or container.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path. Something like "/Users/cs/desktop/test.txt" on Mac and "C:\Programs\Data\test.txt" on Windows. Files without path end in the root directory on Mac.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Can be base64 to decode text first from base64 encoding. Or can be Hex to decode from hex encoding.

examples.label: Write file with some text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.WriteFile"; "Hello World"; $Path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Write file with some text and decode base64:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.WriteFile"; "SGVsbG8gV29ybGQ="; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.txt"; "base64")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Write container to temp file and launch:
examples.text_code:
# get temp folder
Set Variable [
$TempFolder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.SystemTemporary
" )]
# and file name from container
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
test::myContainer
)]
# add name to folder path
Set Variable [
$TempPath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$TempFolder
;
$name
)]
# now write temp file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Files.WriteFile";
test::myContainer
;
$TempPath
)]
# and launch file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.LaunchFile
";
$TempPath
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get temp folder</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.SystemTemporary" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TempFolder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and file name from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; test::myContainer )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add name to folder path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $TempFolder; $name )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TempPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now write temp file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.WriteFile"; test::myContainer; $TempPath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and launch file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.LaunchFile"; $TempPath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$folderpath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $folderpath; "dynapdf.dll" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.WriteFile"; test2::DynaPDFContainer; $libPath )</Calculation>
    </Value>

functions.name: IKPictureTaker.SetNotification
functions.description: Installs a notification script. This script is called whenever the buttons in the panel are called. Parameter to this script is "0" for cancel and "1" for OK. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

examples.label: Installs notification script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "IKPictureTaker.SetNotification"; Get(Filename); "GotImage" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Mutex.Create
functions.description: Creates a new mutex with given name. If mutex exists already, we open it. The mutex lives until the last application closed it. Please call Mutex.Release later. If name matches the name of an existing event, semaphore, waitable timer, job, or file-mapping object, the function fails. You can open the same mutex twice and get different reference numbers from the plugin.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name to use for the mutex. Name should be a simple text, but unique, so we recommend reverse domain notation. The name should not contain a slash.

examples.label: Create a mutex
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Mutex.Create"; "com.monkeybreadsoftware.test" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create the mutex and use it:
examples.text_code:
# create a mutex
Set Variable [
$mutex
; Value:
MBS
( "Mutex.Create"; "de.monkeybreadsoftware.test" ) ]
# lock it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Mutex.TryLock
";
$Mutex
) ]
If [
$r
= 1 ]
# do something
#
# finally unlock
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Mutex.Unlock
";
$Mutex
) ]
End If
# free it mutex
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Mutex.Release
";
$Mutex
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> create a mutex</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Mutex.Create"; "de.monkeybreadsoftware.test" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$mutex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> lock it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Mutex.TryLock"; $Mutex ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $r = 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> do something</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> finally unlock</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Mutex.Unlock"; $Mutex ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free it mutex</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Mutex.Release"; $Mutex ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Mutex.Lock
functions.description: Locks the mutex. Only one application can lock the mutex. Please call Mutex.Unlock later to unlock the mutex. To avoid you looking up the FileMaker app, we limit locking to one minute. After that you get a timeout error.

parameters.name: Mutex
parameters.description: The reference number of the mutex.

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Mutex.Lock"; $Mutex )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Lock and unlock:
examples.text_code:
# lock it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Mutex.Lock";
$Mutex
) ]
# do something
#
# finally unlock
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Mutex.Unlock
";
$Mutex
) ]
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> lock it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Mutex.Lock"; $Mutex ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> do something</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> finally unlock</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Mutex.Unlock"; $Mutex ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

= 1 ]

# finally unlock
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Mutex.Unlock
";
$Mutex
) ]
End If

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

functions.name: Printer.Print
functions.signature: MBS( "Printer.Print"; Printer { ; CloseDialog; Number of Copies; Paper Format; Paper Orientation; Paper Source; Pages From; Pages To; Collate Pages; What; PrintToFile } )
functions.description: Prints a document with options. The plugin will show the print dialog from FileMaker, automatically set options and close dialog if requested. Sub dialog for paper is opened if paper format, paper orientation or source is passed. The function returns before the dialog shows, so please do a script pause after calling this. This function requires the permissions for current user to print and the print menu commands be available via menu as we trigger those to print. You can later call Print. ErrorLog to detect errors. If Windows ever change dialogs, features may no longer work. FileMaker may not show the print dialog if there is no record available or when debugger is open. For Mac, please check PrintDialog.Install function. To print on a server, you can create a PDF document and send it to printer via DynaPDF.Print or PDFKit.Print functions. After calling this method, you can call Printer.PaperFormats , Printer.PaperSources or Printer.PrinterNames to learn what was available. Version 9.1 or newer allows you to pass 3 for CloseDialog parameter to leave dialog open while sub dialog is closed. Original source code sponsored by schubec.com http://www.schubec.com

parameters.name: Printer
parameters.description: The printer name to set. Must not match exactly, just the first letters. If empty, we use current.

parameters.name: CloseDialog
parameters.description: Whether to close print dialog. Pass 0 to leave dialog open. Pass 1 to close by pressing OK. Pass 2 to press cancel button in case you just want to learn the options. Pass 3 to leave dialog open, but close sub dialog.

parameters.name: Number of Copies
parameters.description: The number of copies. Leave empty to not change.

parameters.name: Paper Format
parameters.description: The name of the paper format. Leave empty to not change.

parameters.name: Paper Orientation
parameters.description: The paper orientation. Pass portrait or landscape. Empty value leaves setting as is.

parameters.name: Paper Source
parameters.description: The paper source. Pass name of setting. Name doesn't need to match exactly, just first letters. So you can pass "Man" für Manual (English) and Manuell (German).

parameters.name: Pages From
parameters.description: The start number of page range. Pass empty text for Pages From and To to select all pages.

parameters.name: Pages To
parameters.description: The end number of page range. Pass empty text for Pages From and To to select all pages.

parameters.name: Collate Pages
parameters.description: Whether to collate pages. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable. Other values are ignored, so pass -1 or empty value to leave existing value.

parameters.name: What
parameters.description: What to print: Pass 0 for records being browsed. Pass 1 for current record. Pass 2 for Blank Record showing fields. Default is empty (or -1) to keep current setting.

parameters.name: PrintToFile
parameters.description: Whether to print to file. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable. Other values are ignored, so pass -1 or empty value to leave existing value.

examples.label: Print:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Printer.Print"; Printer Dialog::Printer Name; Printer Dialog::Close Dialog; Printer Dialog::Number of Copies; Printer Dialog::Paper Format; Printer Dialog::Paper Orientation; Printer Dialog::Paper Source; Printer Dialog:…</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>2</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Result"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS("Printer.ErrorLog")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Install special print menu with print command:
examples.text_code:
# set a menu with print commands, so the print works
Install Menu Set [ “[Print]” ; Use as file default: Off ]
# now let the plugin print
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("printer.print") ]
# wait
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): 2]
# and restore custom menus
Install Menu Set [ “[Standard Menu]” ; Use as file default: On ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set a menu with print commands, so the print works</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="142" name="Install Menu Set">
    <UseAsFileDefault state="False"/>
    <CustomMenuSet id="99999" name="[Print]"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now let the plugin print</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ("printer.print") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> wait</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>2</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and restore custom menus</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="142" name="Install Menu Set">
    <UseAsFileDefault state="True"/>
    <DisableStepCollapsed state="False"/>
    <CustomMenuSet id="1" name="[Standard Menu]"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Printer.PrinterCount
functions.description: Queries the number of printers. On Windows we query local and favorite printers.

examples.label: Queries number of printers:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Printer.PrinterCount" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query list of all printers with While loop:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [ 
	count = MBS( "Printer.PrinterCount" ); 
	list = While ( 
		[
			l = ""; 
			i = 0
		]; 
			i < count ; 
		[  
			l = l & MBS( "Printer.PrinterName"; i ) & ¶; 
			i = i + 1 
		] ; l )
	]; list )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Printer.PrinterInfo
functions.description: Queries printer info. Selectors on Mac: Name, Type, languageLevel (PostScript language level or zero). If you ask for deviceDescriptionKeys, you get a list of other keys available. Selectors on Windows: ServerName, PrinterName, ShareName, PortName, DriverName, Comment, Location, SepFile, PrintProcessor, Datatype, Parameters, SepFile, Priority, DefaultPriority, StartTime, UntilTime, Status, Jobs, AveragePPM, Status, Attributes. Attributes and Status are returned as numbers. If you pass "Keys" for selector, we return list of all available keys.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the printer.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: The selector for the value to query.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create records from printer list:
examples.text_code:
# List printers in file Printers
Delete All Records [ With dialog: Off ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
Printer.PrinterCount
" ) ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
New Record/Request
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "Printer.PrinterName";
$index
) ]
Set Field [
Printers::Printer
Name ;
$name
]
#
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
#
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> List printers in file Printers</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="10" name="Delete All Records">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Printer.PrinterCount" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Printer.PrinterName"; $index ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$name </Calculation>
    <Field table="Printers" id="99999" name="Printer Name "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Printer.SetPrinter
functions.signature: MBS( "Printer.SetPrinter"; Printer { ; CloseDialog; Paper Format; Paper Orientation; Paper Source } )
functions.description: Switches printer or changes paper setup. Shows the printer setup dialog and switches target printer or paper settings. Please use a script pause of at least a second after calling this function to let Windows show the dialogs. This function requires the permissions for current user to print and the print menu commands be available via menu as we trigger those to set printer. You can later call Print. ErrorLog to detect errors. If Windows ever change dialogs, features may no longer work. For Mac, please check PrintDialog.Install function. Version 10.0 of MBS Plugin adds three new parameters paper format, orientation and source. And the plugin fills the internal lists for paper sources, formats and printers. Original source code sponsored by schubec.com http://www.schubec.com

parameters.name: Printer
parameters.description: The printer name to set. Must not match exactly, just the first letters. Leave empty to not change printer.

parameters.name: CloseDialog
parameters.description: Whether to close print dialog. Pass 1 to close or 0 to not close. Pass 2 to press cancel button in case you just want to learn the options.

parameters.name: Paper Format
parameters.description: The name of the paper format. Leave empty to not change.

parameters.name: Paper Orientation
parameters.description: The paper orientation. Pass portrait or landscape. Empty value leaves setting as is.

parameters.name: Paper Source
parameters.description: The paper source. Pass name of setting. Name doesn't need to match exactly, just first letters. So you can pass "Man" für Manual (English) and Manuell (German).

examples.label: Switch printer:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Printer.SetPrinter";
Printer Dialog::Printer
Name)]
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): 2]
Show Custom Dialog ["Result";
MBS
("Printer.
ErrorLog
")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Printer.SetPrinter"; Printer Dialog::Printer Name)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>2</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Result"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS("Printer.ErrorLog")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.AvailableBytes
functions.description: Queries number of bytes available in buffer.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

examples.label: Queries bytes and reads them:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.AvailableBytes"; $PortReference )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Read"; $PortReference; $count; "windows" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Read in loop as long as data comes in:
examples.text_code:
# read data until end
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:""]
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
#exit when no more data
Exit Loop If [not (
MBS
( "SerialPort.AvailableBytes";
$port
) > 0)]
#read some data
Set Variable [
$newdata
; Value:
MBS
( "
SerialPort.Read
";
$port
; 10000; "windows" )]
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
$data
&
$newdata
]
#next
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> read data until end</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>exit when no more data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>not (MBS( "SerialPort.AvailableBytes"; $port ) > 0)</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>read some data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Read"; $port; 10000; "windows" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$newdata</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$data & $newdata</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.Clear
functions.description: Clears incoming buffer. Reads all data from serial port.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.name: SerialPort.ClearDataAvailableHandler
functions.description: Clears the data available handler.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.name: SerialPort.Close
functions.description: Closes serial port.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$port</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Write"; $port; "Hello")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Close"; $port )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.CloseAll
functions.description: Closes all serial ports. Release all serial port connection objects from our plugin.

functions.name: SerialPort.GetBaudRate
functions.description: Queries the baud rate.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.result: Returns baud rate as number or error message.

functions.name: SerialPort.GetCTS
functions.description: Queries current CTS mode. If enabled, the CTS (clear-to-send) signal is monitored for output flow control. Than if CTS is turned off, output is suspended until CTS is sent again.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.result: Returns current value (0 or 1) or error message.

functions.name: SerialPort.GetDSR
functions.description: Queries whether DSR mode is used. If enabled DSR (data-set-ready) signal is monitored for output flow control.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.result: Returns current value (0 or 1) or error message.

functions.name: SerialPort.GetDTR
functions.description: Queries whether DTR (data-terminal-ready) flow control is used.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.result: Returns current value (0 or 1) or error message.

functions.name: SerialPort.GetDataBits
functions.description: Queries the data bit size. Value is 5, 6, 7 or 8.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.result: Returns data size as number or error message.

functions.name: SerialPort.GetParity
functions.description: Queries parity setting. Can be 0 (off), 1 (odd) or 2 (even)

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

examples.label: Queries parity:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SerialPort.GetParity"; $SerialPort)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.GetRTS
functions.description: Queries whether to use RTS (request-to-send) flow control.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.result: Returns current value (0 or 1) or error message.

functions.name: SerialPort.GetStopBits
functions.description: Queries number of stop bits. Can be 1 or 2 on Mac and 1, 1.5 or 2 on Windows.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

examples.label: Queries setting:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SerialPort.GetStopBits"; $SerialPort)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.GetTag
functions.description: Queries tag value.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.name: SerialPort.GetXON
functions.description: Queries if XON/XOFF flow control is used.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.result: Returns current value (0 or 1) or error message.

functions.name: SerialPort.HasLine
functions.description: Checks if a line is available. The plugin checks the input buffer for a newline character. The script trigger pauses after it triggered a script and waits for you to call a function to read. This function clears the flag and allows the script trigger to fire again.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.name: SerialPort.IsSuspended
functions.description: Checks suspension state. While suspended, we do not trigger event and clear incoming buffer regularly.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.name: SerialPort.LastError
functions.description: Returns the system error from last serial port function call. The error code is zero for no error. On Windows you get the windows system error codes and on macOS the unix system error codes.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.name: SerialPort.List
functions.description: Returns list of names of serial ports. On Windows can list ports with textual name. When script calling this function runs on client in FileMaker Pro, you may see the ports of the machine your FileMaker Pro runs on. But if run on server side, you see the ports of the server. Added mode 2 for MBS Plugin in version 12.3 to return information as JSON. Returns JSON array with objects. Each object has at least Index and Name values.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Pass 1 on Windows to see longer names from registry. Pass 2 on macOS or Windows to return JSON.

examples.label: Get device list and pick first one:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "SerialPort.List" ) ]
Set Variable [
$firstDevice
; Value: LeftValues(
$list
; 1) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.List" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>LeftValues($list; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$firstDevice</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.Open
functions.description: Opens a serial port. You get the names from SerialPort.List function. You can even open ports, if they are not in the list. The port is opened with default settings. Serial Port reference numbers are starting at 28000 and counting up for each new port. Even if port is not showing in list, it may still open here, e.g. virtual COM ports on Windows. You can open several ports with different connections, e.g. by calling SerialPort.Open once for each connection. Version 9.2 introduced a workaround to make this work for port numbers > COM9 on Windows.

parameters.name: PortName
parameters.description: The name of the serial port to open.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: In case you have several ports with same name, you can specify an index here. Zero for first port with given name, 1 for the second port with given name.

functions.result: Returns the port reference value which you pass to other SerialPort functions.

examples.label: Open port and close it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Open"; "COM1" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$port</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Write"; $port; "Hello")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Close"; $port )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

examples.label: Record weight in predefined field:
examples.text_code:
#Change to reflect actual Layout
Go to Layout [ “MPO201 Input Weight” (MPORawMaterialColourInputs_MPO RM inputs) ]
Set Variable [
$portList
; Value:
MBS
("
SerialPort.List
") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Serial Ports"; Message:
$portList
; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes”; Button 2: “Cancel”, Commit: “No” ]
If [ Get ( LastMessageChoice ) = 2 ]
Exit Script [ ]
End If
#Change to reflect actual COM port
Set Variable [
$port
; Value:"COM4" ]
Set Variable [
$serialPort
; Value:
MBS
("SerialPort.Open";
$port
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Error"; Message: "Serial port error " &
$serialPort
; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” ]
Exit Script [ ]
End If
Set Variable [
$baudSet
; Value:
MBS
("
SerialPort.SetBaudRate
";
$serialPort
; 9600) ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:0 ]
// New Record/Request
Loop
Set Variable [
$newScaleData
; Value:
MBS
("
SerialPort.Read
";
$serialPort
; 1000) ]
Exit Loop If [
$newScaleData
≠ "" ]
Exit Loop If [
$count
> 100 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): .01 ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
$count
+ 1 ]
End Loop
#Change to reflect fields
Set Field [
MPORawMaterialColourInputs_MPO RM inputs::mpoiw
Input Weight Grams;
$newScaleData
]
Set Field [
MPORawMaterialColourInputs_MPO RM inputs::mpoiw
Input Weight Grams; Trim ( Left (
$newScaleData
; 9) ) ]
Commit Records/Requests
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SerialPort.Close
";
$serialPort
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Error"; Message: "Serial port error " &
$result
; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Info"; Message: "Serial port closed " &
$result
; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Change to reflect actual Layout</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="MPO201 Input Weight"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SerialPort.List") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$portList</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> Title: "Serial Ports"; Message: $portList; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes”</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>Button 2: “Cancel”, Commit: “No” </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Get ( LastMessageChoice ) = 2 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Change to reflect actual COM port</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"COM4" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$port</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SerialPort.Open"; $port) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$serialPort</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> Title: "Error"; Message: "Serial port error " & $serialPort</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SerialPort.SetBaudRate"; $serialPort; 9600) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$baudSet</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="False" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SerialPort.Read"; $serialPort; 1000) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$newScaleData</Name>

  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $newScaleData ≠ "" </Calculation>

  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">

  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">

    <Calculation>.01 </Calculation>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Repetition>

    <Calculation>Trim ( Left ( $newScaleData ; 9) ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MPORawMaterialColourInputs_MPO RM inputs" id="99999" name="mpoiw Input Weight Grams"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>

      <Calculation>MBS("SerialPort.Close"; $serialPort) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

    </Title>

    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>

        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

functions.name: SerialPort.OpenPath
functions.description: Opens a special file, device or pipe. This allows you to e.g. open a pipe on Windows. Serial Port reference numbers are starting at 28000 and counting up for each new port.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to open.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The opening mode. Can be Read, Write or ReadWrite. Default is ReadWrite.

functions.result: Returns the port reference value which you pass to other SerialPort functions.

examples.label: Open pipe and send message:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$serial
; Value:
MBS
( "SerialPort.OpenPath"; "\\\.\pipe\Test") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SerialPort.Write
";
$serial
; "Hello World") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SerialPort.Close
";
$serial
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.OpenPath"; "\\\.\pipe\Test") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$serial</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Write"; $serial; "Hello World") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Close"; $serial) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.Path
functions.description: Queries the path used to open the serial port. This is the value passed to SerialPort.Open function. Value is e.g. "COM1"

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.name: SerialPort.Read
functions.description: Reads bytes from serial port and returns them as text. The text returned may be shorter than length bytes. You may need to put in script pauses between sending a request and reading the response as the other device may need a second to process the request, answer and for the answer to be received. See also SerialPort.AvailableBytes function. The script trigger pauses after it triggered a script and waits for you to call a function to read. This function clears the flag and allows the script trigger to fire again.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.description: Maximum number of bytes to read.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Read up to 8 bytes and decode them from Windows text encoding into FileMaker:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Read"; $port; 8; "windows" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Read in loop as long as data comes in:
examples.text_code:
# read data until end
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:""]
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
#exit when no more data
Exit Loop If [not (
MBS
( "
SerialPort.AvailableBytes
";
$port
) > 0)]
#read some data
Set Variable [
$newdata
; Value:
MBS
( "SerialPort.Read";
$port
; 10000; "windows" )]
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
$data
&
$newdata
]
#next
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
End Loop

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>exit when no more data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>not (MBS( "SerialPort.AvailableBytes"; $port ) > 0)</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>read some data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.Read"; $port; 10000; "windows" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$newdata</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$data & $newdata</Calculation>
    </Value>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>next</Text>

Exit Script [ ]

Exit Loop If [

Set Variable [

functions.name: SerialPort.ReadByte
functions.description: Reads a single byte to the serial port. This is very useful for reading control characters which you can't easily put in a text in FileMaker. Like 13 for a LF (line feed) or 10 for CR (carriage return). See also SerialPort.AvailableBytes function. The script trigger pauses after it triggered a script and waits for you to call a function to read. This function clears the flag and allows the script trigger to fire again.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.result: Returns the byte value or an error message.

examples.label: Read one byte:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.ReadByte"; $port )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.ReadHex
functions.description: Reads bytes from serial port and returns them hex encoded. The text returned may be shorter than length bytes. See also SerialPort.AvailableBytes function. The script trigger pauses after it triggered a script and waits for you to call a function to read. This function clears the flag and allows the script trigger to fire again.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.description: Maximum number of bytes to read.

functions.result: Returns text encoded as hex or error message.

examples.label: Read up to 8 bytes and return them as:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.ReadHex"; $port; 8 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.ReadLine
functions.description: Reads a line of text from serial port. The plugin buffers input and reads text until first newline character. Returns text without that newline character. You may need to put in script pauses between sending a request and reading the response as the other device may need a second to process the request, answer and for the answer to be received. The script trigger pauses after it triggered a script and waits for you to call a function to read. This function clears the flag and allows the script trigger to fire again.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Pass hex or base64 to get text back encoded. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: SerialPort.Resume
functions.description: Resumes serial port. While suspended, we do not trigger event and clear incoming buffer regularly.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

functions.name: SerialPort.SetBaudRate

parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Value

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.SetDataAvailableEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the data available expression for this serial port. When new data arrives, this expression is evaluated, so you can call SerialPort.Read functions. Be aware that script may be called when just a few bytes are in the read buffer. So the data message you wait for may only be available partly. In that case, please read available bytes, check if whole packet/message arrived and if not, store read data for later and wait for next evaluation. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the serial port reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

examples.label: Run script with 2 second delay:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.SetDataAvailableEvaluate"; $$Port; 
   "MBS(\"FM.RunScriptLater\"; 2; Get(FileName); \"CheckSerial\")")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.SetDataAvailableHandler
functions.description: Sets the data available handler for this serial port. When new data arrives, this script is called, so you can call SerialPort.Read functions. We pass the port reference as parameter to the script. Be aware that script may be called when just a few bytes are in the read buffer. So the data message you wait for may only be available partly. In that case, please read available bytes, check if whole packet/message arrived and if not, store read data for later and wait for next script call. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: PortReference
parameters.description: The reference number of the port. You get this number from SerialPort.Open when you open the port.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Set data available handler script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SerialPort.SetDataAvailableHandler"; $port; Get(FileName); "SerialPortDataAvailable" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SerialPort.Suspend
functions.description: Suspends serial port. While suspended, we do not trigger event and clear incoming buffer regularly. Please call SerialPort.Resume later.

parameters.name: PortReference

functions.name: SerialPort.WaitNamedPipe
functions.description: Waits for a named pipe. Waits until either a time-out interval elapses or an instance of the specified named pipe is available for connection (that is, the pipe's server process has a pending ConnectNamedPipe operation on the pipe). Returns OK if pipe is available or returns an error.

parameters.name: NamedPipeName
parameters.description: The name of the named pipe. The string must include the name of the computer on which the server process is executing. A period may be used for the servername if the pipe is local.

parameters.name: TimeOut
parameters.description: The number of milliseconds that the function will wait for an instance of the named pipe to be available. We limit this from 0 to 5000. Default is 100ms.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SerialPort.WaitNamedPipe"; "\\\\.\\pipe\\test"; 100)
// all backslashes doubled so FileMaker passes them</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
