functions.name: SetSystemUIMode
functions.description: Sets the system UI mode. The System UI Mode controls the presentation of system-provided user interface elements. Note that the system UI mode is a per-process state. Switching from a process that is suppressing system UI to another process that is not will cause system UI elements to become visible automatically. Switching back to the first process will suppress system UI elements again. In this mode, all standard system UI elements are visible. kUIModeNormal = 0 In this mode, system UI elements which cover the "content area" of the screen (the area other than the menubar) are hidden. However, these elements may automatically show themselves in response to mouse movements or other user activity; specifically, the Dock will still show itself automatically when the mouse moves into the Dock's auto-show region. kUIModeContentSuppressed = 1 In this mode, system UI elements which cover the "content area" of the screen (the area other than the menubar) are hidden. Unlike kUIModeContentSuppressed, most UI elements will not automatically show themselves in this mode. kUIModeContentHidden = 2, In this mode, all system UI elements, including the menubar, are hidden. However, these elements may automatically show themselves in response to mouse movements or other user activity; specifically, the Dock and menubar will still show themselves automatically when the mouse moves into the Dock's auto-show region. Available in macOS 10.3 and later. kUIModeAllSuppressed = 4, In this mode, all system UI elements, including the menubar, are hidden. Most system UI elements will not automatically show themselves in this mode. The application may request that the menubar automatically show itself while in this mode by passing the kUIOptionAutoShowMenuBar flag to SetSystemUIMode. kUIModeAllHidden = 3 Options which you can combine by adding them together: Requests that the menubar automatically show itself when the user moves the mouse into the screen area that would ordinarily be occupied by the menubar. Only valid with kUIModeAllHidden. kUIOptionAutoShowMenuBar = 1 Disables all items in the Apple menu. Valid for all modes. kUIOptionAnimateMenuBar = 2 Requests that the menubar animate on or offscreen, if the UIMode is also being changed such that the menubar will change visibility. Unlike other UI options, this option is not stored and only affects the behavior of this call to SetSystemUIMode. Valid for all modes. Available in Mac OS X 10.7 and later. kUIOptionDisableAppleMenu = 4 The active application may not be changed while this process is active. Currently disables the Command-Tab and Command-Shift-Tab key sequences to switch the active process, and the global window rotation key sequence selected by the user in the Keyboard preference pane. SetFrontProcess may still be used to explicitly switch the active process. Only valid with modes other than kUIModeNormal. kUIOptionDisableProcessSwitch = 8 The Force Quit window may not be displayed while this process is active. Currently disables the Command-Option-Escape key sequence to open the Force Quit window and the Force Quit menu item in the Apple menu. Only valid with modes other than kUIModeNormal. kUIOptionDisableForceQuit = 16 The current login session may not be terminated while this process is active. Currently disables the Power key and the Restart, Shut Down, and Log Out menu items in the Apple menu. Only valid with modes other than kUIModeNormal. kUIOptionDisableSessionTerminate = 32 The Hide menu item in the Application menu is disabled. Note that this option does not prevent this application from being hidden if Hide Others is selected in some other application. Available in Mac OS X 10.3 and later. kUIOptionDisableHide = 64 Provides the ability to specify whether the menu bar will disable its adaptive transparency. Note this should be used in conjunction with a full-screen window using kUIModeContentSuppressed or kUIModeContentHidden. Available in macOS 10.6 and later. This constant was published in the Mac OS X 10.5 version of MacApplication.h, but had the wrong value (7) and should not be used in Mac OS X 10.5. kUIOptionDisableMenuBarTransparency = 512

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The mode you want.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options you want.

examples.label: Sets mode with hidden content:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SetSystemUIMode"; 1; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.description: The reference number to the model.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the input feature.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The image file path.

examples.label: Bind an image:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WindowsML.BindImageFile";
$$WinMLRef
; "data_0";
WindowsML::Image
File Path) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to set image" ;
$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsML.BindImageFile"; $$WinMLRef; "data_0"; WindowsML::Image File Path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to set image" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: $r </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WindowsML.Description"; 73001)</Calculation>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>

functions.name: WindowsML.Evaluate
functions.description: Evaluates the model and returns result as JSON. Returned JSON contains an entry for each output key and in that the values. We can currently serialize arrays for boolean, float, float16, double, int8, uint8, int16, uint16, int32, uint32, int64 and uint64 data types. MBS Plugins don't support all possible input/output combinations, so if you need something more, please let us know.

parameters.name: WinMLRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the model.

parameters.name: correlationId
parameters.description: Optional user-supplied string to connect the output results. Will be included in JSON for output.

examples.label: Evaluate the model:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsML.Evaluate"; $$WinMLRef) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to evaluate model" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$Result </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: $Result </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$Result </Calculation>
    <Field table="WindowsML" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsML.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of ML objects. Returns IDs for all Windows ML objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call WindowsML.Release in your scripts.

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsML.Open
functions.description: Loads a model for Windows Machine Learning. Please use an onnx file as you find them on various websites, e.g. start here: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/ai/windows-ml/get-onnx-model Will return an error about unregistered class if called on older Windows versions before 10. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the model file.

examples.label: Load a model
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$model
; Value:
MBS
("WindowsML.Open"; "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\SqueezeNet.onnx") ]
Example result:
73001
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WindowsML.Open"; "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\SqueezeNet.onnx") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$model</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Load a model and show description:
examples.text_code:
# Load Model in file WindowsML
Go to Layout [ “WindowsML” (WindowsML) ; Animation: None ]
#
Set Variable [
$WinMLRef
; Value:
MBS
( "WindowsML.Open";
WindowsML::Model
File Path ) ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load model" ;
$WinMLRef
]
Else
Set Variable [
$$WinMLRef
; Value:
$WinMLRef
]
Set Field [
WindowsML::Model
Description ;
MBS
("
JSON.Colorize
";
MBS
( "
WindowsML.Description
";
$$WinMLRef
)) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load Model in file WindowsML</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="WindowsML"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsML.Open"; WindowsML::Model File Path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$WinMLRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to load model" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$WinMLRef </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$WinMLRef </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$WinMLRef</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Colorize"; MBS( "WindowsML.Description"; $$WinMLRef )) </Calculation>
    <Field table="WindowsML" id="99999" name="Model Description "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

Windows User Notifications::ID

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.Release
functions.description: Releases the notification. This frees up memory used.

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

examples.label: Free notification:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WindowsUserNotification.Release";
Windows User Notifications::ID
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error";
$r
]
End If
Set Field [
Windows User Notifications::ID
; ""]
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>""</Calculation>
    <Field table="Windows User Notifications" id="99999" name="ID"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetExpiresOnReboot
functions.description: Sets whether the toast notification will remain in the Notification Center after a reboot. If 1, notification will expire when the computer is rebooted. Requires Windows 10, version 1903 (introduced in 10.0.18362.0) Value is -1 to take Windows’ default setting.

parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetFileName

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetImagePath
functions.description: Sets image path for the image to show. May not be a network path as system needs to load it independent of your permissions. Maybe better use image coming with application or put one in temp folder. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native file path for the image.

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetNotificationMirroring
functions.description: Sets a value that specifies whether notification mirroring is allowed. Requires Windows 10 Anniversary Edition (introduced in 10.0.14393.0) Pass 0 to allow or 1 to disallow. Value is -1 to take Windows’ default setting.

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

parameters.name: Value

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetPriority
functions.description: Sets the priority of the toast notification. Requires Windows 10 Creators Update (introduced in 10.0.15063.0) The priority setting provides hints on how and at what urgency level a notification should be presented to the user (whether to wake up the screen, etc). Whether the notification is displayed in high priority is based on the state and power management policy of the device. Pass 1 for high and 0 for normal. Value is -1 to take Windows’ default setting.

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting.

examples.label: Set to high priority:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetRemoteId
functions.description: Sets a remote id. A remote id for the notification that enables the system to correlate this notification with another one generated on another device. Requires Windows 10 Anniversary Edition (introduced in 10.0.14393.0)

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new remote ID.

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetScriptName
functions.description: Sets the script name for script trigger. The parameter to the script includes a list of values. First entry in the list is the notification reference number. Second entry is the reason to call which can be Activated, Dismissed or Failed. Third value is reason for demission or error code for failure. When called for Dismissed, we pass an additional value for the reason: UserCanceled, ApplicationHidden or TimedOut. When called for Failed, we pass the error code as additional parameter.

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Set which script to trigger:

$r
; Value:

( "WindowsUserNotification.SetScriptName";
$ID
; "NotificationTrigger") ]

$r
; Value:

( "
WindowsUserNotification.SetFileName
";
$ID
; Get(FileName)) ]
examples.xml_code:

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetSuppressPopup
functions.description: Sets whether a toast's pop-up UI is displayed on the user's screen. Set to 1 to suppress the popup message; otherwise, 0. The default value is 0, meaning the toast's pop-up message will be shown. Setting this property to true places the toast notification silently into the action center. This enables your app to communicate with the user without interrupting them. Value is -1 to take Windows’ default setting.

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting.

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the unique identifier of this notification within the notification Group. This API returns an error if the property is set to null or the empty string. The tag can be maximum 16 characters long. However, the Creators Update (15063) extends this limit to 64 characters. e.g. could be the record ID for the notification in your database.

parameters.name: Notification
parameters.description: The reference number for the Windows User Notification.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new tag value.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.SetText

parameters.name: Text

functions.name: WindowsUserNotification.Setting
functions.description: Queries notification status. Gets a value that tells you whether there is an app, user, or system block that prevents the display of a notification. If allowed, you get back "Enabled" as setting. When notifications have been disabled at more than one level, this property value reflects the setting with the largest scope. Precedence is as follows, from largest scope to smallest: DisabledByManifest DisabledByGroupPolicy DisabledForUser DisabledForApplication If toast notifications have been disabled both by the user and by group policy, this property returns DisabledByGroupPolicy.

If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

functions.result: Returns X509 reference number or error.

; Value:

("IsError")]

$cert
]

Show Custom Dialog ["Name";
MBS
( "X509.Name";
$cert
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "X509.Release";
$cert
)]

functions.name: X509.Release

parameters.name: X509Ref

functions.name: X509.ReleaseAll

functions.name: X509.ShowDialog
functions.description: Shows standard dialog with details. Displays the content of the certificate in the standard dialog to user. Implemented for Windows with plugins version 11.1.

parameters.name: X509Ref
parameters.description: The reference to the X509 certificate.

examples.label: Show certificate:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$cert
; Value:
MBS
( "X509.Read";
Zertifikat::Certificate
Text ) ]
Set Variable [
$serial
; Value:
MBS
( "X509.ShowDialog";
$cert
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "X509.Release";
$cert
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "X509.Release"; $cert) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: X509.Write
functions.description: Writes certificate. Provides either PEM file as text or DER file base64 encoded.

parameters.name: X509Ref
parameters.description: The reference to the X509 certificate.

parameters.name: Binary
parameters.description: Whether to return binary representation (DER format) instead of text (PEM format). Default is PEM.

examples.label: Calculate x5t for a certificate file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
// read PEM file
cert = MBS( "X509.ReadFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Downloads/Cert.pem" );
// write DER file
data = MBS( "X509.Write"; cert; 1 );
// and free memory
r = MBS( "X509.Release"; cert );
// hash it to get x5t
hash = MBS( "Hash.Digest"; "SHA1"; "Base64"; data; ""; "base64url")
]; hash )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
